   "outputs": [],
   "source": [
    "# Install gwexpy with pinned versions of core dependencies for reproducibility on Colab\n",
    "\n",
    "%pip install -q \"gwexpy[all]\" \"gwpy<5.0.0\" \"numpy<2.0.0\" \"scipy<1.13.0\" \"astropy<7.0.0\"\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "87bb92e2",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "# Control Analysis: Discretization Basics\n",
    "\n",
    "[![Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/assets/colab-badge.svg)](https://colab.research.google.com/github/tatsuki-washimi/gwexpy/blob/main/docs/web/en/user_guide/tutorials/advanced_control_discretization.ipynb)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "6a01b80f",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "This notebook promotes the legacy control-theory example into the public docs set. The point is not just to call `c2d()`: it is to show how a continuous plant changes when a digital controller samples and holds actuator commands.\n",
    "\n",
    "We compare **Zero-Order Hold (ZOH)** and **Tustin** because they preserve different physical aspects of the loop. ZOH is faithful to DAC-held commands in time-domain actuation, while Tustin tends to preserve frequency response near crossover more evenly."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 1,
   "id": "a67d9c81",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-20T16:27:01.284113Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-20T16:27:01.283930Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.144536Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.143961Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "<TransferFunction>: sys[0]\n",
      "Inputs (1): ['u[0]']\n",
      "Outputs (1): ['y[0]']\n",
      "\n",
      "      1\n",
      "  ---------\n",
      "  0.5 s + 1\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "import matplotlib.pyplot as plt\n",
    "import numpy as np\n",
    "from control.matlab import bode, c2d, step, tf\n",
    "\n",
    "from gwexpy import FrequencySeries, TimeSeries\n",
    "\n",
    "# Use a simple first-order plant so the discretization trade-off is easy to interpret physically.\n",
    "P = tf([0, 1], [0.5, 1])\n",
    "print(P)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "2b3b4c23",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 1. Discretize the plant\n",
    "\n",
    "Sampling does two things at once: it freezes actuator commands between updates and it warps the frequency axis seen by the digital filter. Those are different physical approximations, so we keep both versions."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 2,
   "id": "dad60f3c",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.232772Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.145863Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.238504Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.237292Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "name": "stdout",
     "output_type": "stream",
     "text": [
      "ZOH:\n",
      "<TransferFunction>: sys[0]$sampled\n",
      "Inputs (1): ['u[0]']\n",
      "Outputs (1): ['y[0]']\n",
      "dt = 0.2\n",
      "\n",
      "    0.3297\n",
      "  ----------\n",
      "  z - 0.6703\n",
      "Tustin:\n",
      "<TransferFunction>: sys[0]$sampled\n",
      "Inputs (1): ['u[0]']\n",
      "Outputs (1): ['y[0]']\n",
      "dt = 0.2\n",
      "\n",
      "  0.1667 z + 0.1667\n",
      "  -----------------\n",
      "     z - 0.6667\n"
     ]
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "ts = 0.2  # Sampling time [s]\n",
    "\n",
    "# ZOH assumes the DAC holds each command constant until the next sample, which is often the right actuator model.\n",
    "Pd_zoh = c2d(P, ts, method=\"zoh\")\n",
    "\n",
    "# Tustin maps the continuous pole/zero structure through a bilinear transform, which usually preserves loop-shape better near crossover.\n",
    "Pd_tustin = c2d(P, ts, method=\"tustin\")\n",
    "\n",
    "print(\"ZOH:\")\n",
    "print(Pd_zoh)\n",
    "print(\"Tustin:\")\n",
    "print(Pd_tustin)\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "b77a0279",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 2. Compare frequency response\n",
    "\n",
    "The physically important question is where phase and gain begin to drift away from the continuous plant. Excess phase error near the intended unity-gain region directly changes stability margin."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 3,
   "id": "8f8b602d",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.239780Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.239665Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.945127Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.943974Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure 1>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure 2>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure 3>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure 4>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "omega = np.logspace(-2, 2, 100)\n",
    "mag_c, phase_c, w_c = bode(P, omega, plot=False)\n",
    "mag_z, phase_z, w_z = bode(Pd_zoh, omega, plot=False)\n",
    "mag_t, phase_t, w_t = bode(Pd_tustin, omega, plot=False)\n",
    "\n",
    "fs_mag_c = FrequencySeries(20 * np.log10(mag_c), frequencies=w_c, unit=\"dB\", name=\"Continuous\")\n",
    "fs_mag_z = FrequencySeries(20 * np.log10(mag_z), frequencies=w_z, unit=\"dB\", name=\"ZOH\")\n",
    "fs_mag_t = FrequencySeries(20 * np.log10(mag_t), frequencies=w_t, unit=\"dB\", name=\"Tustin\")\n",
    "\n",
    "fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(10, 8), sharex=True)\n",
    "fs_mag_c.plot(ax=ax1, label=\"Continuous\")\n",
    "fs_mag_z.plot(ax=ax1, label=\"ZOH\", linestyle=\"--\")\n",
    "fs_mag_t.plot(ax=ax1, label=\"Tustin\", linestyle=\":\")\n",
    "ax1.set_xscale(\"log\")\n",
    "ax1.set_ylabel(\"Magnitude [dB]\")\n",
    "ax1.legend()\n",
    "ax1.grid(which=\"both\", alpha=0.5)\n",
    "\n",
    "ax2.semilogx(w_c, np.rad2deg(phase_c), label=\"Continuous\")\n",
    "ax2.semilogx(w_z, np.rad2deg(phase_z), label=\"ZOH\", linestyle=\"--\")\n",
    "ax2.semilogx(w_t, np.rad2deg(phase_t), label=\"Tustin\", linestyle=\":\")\n",
    "ax2.set_ylabel(\"Phase [deg]\")\n",
    "ax2.set_xlabel(\"Frequency [rad/s]\")\n",
    "ax2.legend()\n",
    "ax2.grid(which=\"both\", alpha=0.5)\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "markdown",
   "id": "115c3b2e",
   "metadata": {},
   "source": [
    "## 3. Compare time response\n",
    "\n",
    "Time-domain comparisons reveal the other half of the story: ZOH reproduces held actuator commands naturally, while Tustin may look better in loop-shape analysis but can misrepresent the exact held-input waveform."
   ]
  },
  {
   "cell_type": "code",
   "execution_count": 4,
   "id": "0e8d4811",
   "metadata": {
    "execution": {
     "iopub.execute_input": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.947430Z",
     "iopub.status.busy": "2026-04-20T16:27:03.946955Z",
     "iopub.status.idle": "2026-04-20T16:27:41.361266Z",
     "shell.execute_reply": "2026-04-20T16:27:41.360156Z"
    }
   },
   "outputs": [
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure 1>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure 2>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    },
    {
     "data": {
      "image/png": "",
      "text/plain": [
       "<Figure 3>"
      ]
     },
     "metadata": {},
     "output_type": "display_data"
    }
   ],
   "source": [
    "T_end = 3\n",
    "Tc = np.arange(0, T_end, 0.01)\n",
    "Td = np.arange(0, T_end, ts)\n",
    "\n",
    "# Step response is a direct check of how the held-input assumption changes the plant's apparent settling behavior.\n",
    "yc, tc = step(P, Tc)\n",
    "yd_z, td_z = step(Pd_zoh, Td)\n",
    "yd_t, td_t = step(Pd_tustin, Td)\n",
    "\n",
    "ts_c = TimeSeries(yc, times=tc, unit=\"V\", name=\"Continuous\")\n",
    "ts_z = TimeSeries(yd_z, times=td_z, unit=\"V\", name=\"ZOH\")\n",
    "ts_t = TimeSeries(yd_t, times=td_t, unit=\"V\", name=\"Tustin\")\n",
    "\n",
    "plot = ts_c.plot(label=\"Continuous\", title=\"Step Response Comparison\")\n",
    "ax = plot.gca()\n",
    "ts_z.plot(ax=ax, label=\"ZOH\", marker=\"o\", linestyle=\"None\")\n",
    "ts_t.plot(ax=ax, label=\"Tustin\", marker=\"x\", linestyle=\"None\")\n",
    "ax.legend()\n",
    "ax.grid(True)\n",
    "plt.show()\n"
   ]
  }
 ],
